Title: Dual-Layer Transcriptional-Protein Regulation by HvAP2-12 Represses HvAP2-18 Activity to Fine-Tune Barley Starch Synthesis

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF family transcription factor with two AP2/ERF domains
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low at 5 DPA endosperm, peaks at 10–20 DPA, declines at 25 DPA
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major determinant of barley grain quality, processing quality, hydrolysis characteristics, and yield-related grain composition; amylose-rich starch linked to slower digestibility/resistant starch, amylopectin-rich starch useful for brewing/adhesives
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional regulation of starch biosynthesis and starch composition via repression of another regulator
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of HvAP2-18 promoter; protein-protein interaction that inhibits HvAP2-18 DNA-binding/activation activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses HvAP2-18 expression; indirectly suppresses HvAGP-S1 and HvSBE1 activation by inhibiting HvAP2-18
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as an upstream metaregulator that fine-tunes starch synthesis and amylose/amylopectin balance by repressing HvAP2-18 at transcriptional and protein levels
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; native promoter-driven overexpression; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; split-luciferase; subcellular localization; double overexpression/double mutant analysis; metabolite/starch profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression decreases total starch; knockout increases total starch and significantly decreases amylose; no major effect on grain morphology; modulates starch crystallinity
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HvAP2-12 is an AP2/ERF transcription factor expressed during mid grain development. 2. HvAP2-12 negatively regulates barley grain starch accumulation and affects amylose content. 3. It does not directly activate major starch synthesis gene promoters. 4. It directly represses HvAP2-18 promoter activity and physically interacts with HvAP2-18 protein. 5. Through this dual-layer repression, HvAP2-12 suppresses HvAP2-18-mediated activation of starch genes, fine-tuning starch composition.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 15 DPA seeds; data available on request, no public accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression lowers starch content; no obvious penalties in grain size, grain width/length, hundred-grain weight, or starch granule morphology were observed in this study
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to tailor amylose/amylopectin ratio and starch functionality. Loss-of-function may increase total starch and alter amylose for functional foods; can be co-manipulated with HvAP2-18 for precision starch engineering while preserving granule architecture
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologue of rice OsGL6. Functions mainly as an upstream metaregulator rather than direct activator of starch enzyme genes; discussion notes maintenance of starch homeostasis may involve compensatory pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter-driven overexpression for transgenic HvAP2-12 lines
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mid-stage grain/endosperm expression peak at 10–20 DPA

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF family transcription factor; RSR1 orthologue
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in developing grains; functional analyses focused on 15 DPA grains/endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major determinant of grain starch yield, composition, processing quality, fermentation value, and nutritional functionality
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Direct transcriptional activation of starch biosynthesis genes and control of starch composition/accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation by direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates HvAGP-S1 and HvSBE1 expression by binding their promoters; promotes expression of multiple starch synthesis genes in transcriptome analyses
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms homodimer; forms heterodimer/protein complex with HvAP2-12
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Primary executor of this regulatory module, directly activating key starch synthesis genes to increase starch accumulation and influence amylose/amylopectin composition
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; split-luciferase; subcellular localization; double mutant/double overexpression analysis; starch phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression generally increases total starch and can reduce amylose; mutants decrease total starch and significantly increase amylose; also affects grain length and hundred-grain weight
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HvAP2-18 is a nuclear AP2/ERF transcription factor that forms homodimers. 2. Overexpression generally increases total starch, whereas knockout decreases starch and increases amylose. 3. HvAP2-18 directly binds HvAGP-S1 and HvSBE1 promoters and activates their expression. 4. HvAP2-12 represses HvAP2-18 transcription and inhibits its promoter-binding activity through protein interaction. 5. Genetic interaction analyses show HvAP2-18 is the dominant downstream effector in this starch regulatory pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 15 DPA grains; data available on request, no public accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression can reduce grain length and hundred-grain weight in most lines despite increased starch; mutants alter grain size and reduce starch
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for increasing starch yield in barley for feed, brewing, and bioethanol. Can be deployed alone to raise starch or combined with HvAP2-12 manipulation to customize amylose/amylopectin ratio and processing quality
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as barley orthologue of rice RSR1 in text. Binds GCC-box and STRE cis-elements in HvSBE1 promoter; promoter truncation localized binding-preferred regions in HvAGP-S1 and HvSBE1.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression lines generated; exact promoter not specified in provided text
